name: Jenkins v. Hutton
question: Did the Sixth Circuit err in holding that it could review the merits of a criminal defendant's procedurally defaulted claim in order to prevent a fundamental miscarriage of justice?
description: A case in which the Court determined that, under the circumstances of the particular case, the Sixth Circuit in a habeas proceeding should not have reviewed the merits of a criminal defendant's procedurally defaulted claim.
